Explanation: Using selectFrom(CUSTOMER) fetches all columns from the CUSTOMER table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Consider using select() with only the specific columns needed for this operation.

The `containsIgnoreCase` method performs a LIKE '%value%' search which cannot use indexes efficiently and will cause full table scans on large datasets. Consider using a dedicated full-text search solution like PostgreSQL's tsvector/tsquery, Elasticsearch, or database-specific full-text indexes.

The `notContainsIgnoreCase` method performs a NOT LIKE '%value%' search which cannot leverage indexes and results in slow full table scans. Consider using a full-text search engine or restructuring the query to use indexed columns for filtering.
